programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Once the stately home of Lord Manners, Avon Tyrrell in the New Forest is a fine example of an arts and crafts calendar house. Eric Knowles learns about its fascinating history.

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: The pitches are for an oat-powered snack range, a father-and-son duo's DIY gadget, real-world hide-and-seek adventures, and a sun-shielding lip care range.

programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the day's four Premier League games. Two teams vying for the Champions League places face each other as Manchester United welcome Aston Villa to Old Trafford whilst struggling Tottenham Hotspur travel to Anfield to take on Liverpool. Elsewhere, Crystal Palace play Leeds, and Nottingham Forest entertain Fulham.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Contestants compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the day's game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers
programme.desc: The biking Bakers commandeer a windmill in Lincolnshire to mill their own flour and make a classic brown loaf to go with the perfect ploughman's lunch. Back in Dave's kitchen, they bake a fantastic brown ale and cheese bread, served with onion soup.

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: Explore how aerial photography has revealed Scotland's hidden ancient past and uncovered buried secrets. From an Aberdeenshire field where the very concept of time emerged, through the ghostly outline of a lost ornamental garden to the discovery of hundreds of pizza ovens that once fed Rome's mighty legions, presenter James Crawford reveals how ancestors have written their story on Scotland's landscape.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Wool embroidery, a Beatrix Potter book and Masonic porcelain highlight this edition from Selby in North Yorkshire.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Joining Presenter Paul Martin in the university city of Bangor are experts Mark Stacey and Adam Partridge. Among the items they value are a boxed collection of toys and a rather special mirror. Paul also takes the chance to delve into Wales's cultural past and discovers the charms of a medieval instrument called a crwth.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Steve Backshall, Steve Pemberton, Sam Quek and Rachel Riley test their general knowledge skills on this week's Richard Osman's House of Games: Champions.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay, with unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton, challenges Susan Calman, Max Fosh and Noreen Khan to separate the factual from the far-fetched as the quiz arrives in Dundee.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Leaving Kinlochleven - once known as the electric village because of its links to power-hungry aluminium smelting - Paul discovers a forgotten German PoW camp from WWI in the woods nearby. Taking an old military road out of the village, Paul climbs into the surrounding hills and arrives at the Blackwater dam - a colossal memorial in concrete to the last of the navvies. After visiting the graves of some who died during its construction, Paul treks across empty moorland to a lonely bothy.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions in another semi-final. The specialist subjects are Jeff Buckley, the managerial career of Rafael Benítez, Andy Warhol and the classic Routemaster bus.

programme.title: University Challenge

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the world's longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kate calls Nick lucky as he is already starting to feel quite comfortable after their move northwards. Nick pursues a moorland flasher and drunken hikers, while Kate encounters abuse when she helps a woman with a bullying husband, but it's a bullying cleaner who makes the coppers fume.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Vera Stanhope must unravel the circumstances of self-styled entrepreneur Freddie Gill's death when his body is found by bailiffs, attempting to repossess his house. Freddie is estranged from his wife and son, who have returned to living with her formidable mother Tina, her volatile brother Lee and her taciturn uncle Darren at the family's beloved pub. Freddie's legacy of unpaid debts and ruined lives leaves no shortage of suspects.
